title: Monsoon Over Kaziranga
description: A brooding study of storm light breaking over the grasslands. Bhardwaj works wet-on-wet, letting the greys bleed into the greens so the horizon never quite settles.

title: Chandni Chowk 6 AM
description: The old quarter before it wakes. Menon paints the shutters closed and the light flat, an unusually quiet reading of a place usually shown at full noise.

title: Untitled (Blue Series III)
description: Third in an ongoing series. Layers of indigo pigment and torn paper, sanded back until the substrate shows through in places.

title: The Weaver's Hands
description: A close study of a Paithani weaver at work. Kadam spent three months in Yeola drawing from life.

title: Backwaters at Dusk
description: Kerala backwaters rendered almost abstractly. The palm line dissolves into its own reflection.

title: Ganesh Chaturthi Procession
description: Crowd as colour field. Iyer refuses individual faces, letting the mass of bodies carry the movement instead.

title: Concrete Lullaby
description: A half-built Mumbai tower at night, scaffolding lit from within. Menon's largest work to date.

title: Grandmother's Almirah
description: Fabric scraps from the artist's family embedded into paint. An inventory of inheritance.

title: Kathakali Study No. 4
description: One of nine drawings made backstage in Thrissur. The performer is caught mid-makeup, half in character.

title: Salt Pans Rann
description: A long horizontal format for a long horizontal landscape. Almost nothing in it, and that is the point.

title: Ficus
description: A precise botanical rendering of a banyan aerial root system. Rao trained as a scientific illustrator.

title: Ficus II
description: Companion piece to Ficus. Same tree, two years later.

title: Red Wall Fort Kochi
description: An early Menon. The palette is hotter and the handling looser than his recent work.

title: Anatomy of a Rainstorm
description: Bhardwaj's newest. Rain rendered as vertical structure rather than atmosphere.

title: Threshold
description: A doorway that leads nowhere, built from layered plaster and pigment. Qureshi's first work at this scale.
